name: AP and IB Exam Credit: How BYU Awards Credit
text:
AP and IB Exam Credit: How BYU Awards Credit -- BYU Registrar rules for Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) exam credit:
Advanced Placement (AP)
If you scored a 3-5 on an AP exam, you may be eligible for BYU credit, depending on when you took the exam.
If you would like to send your AP scores to BYU, you can:
At the time of testing, indicate that your scores should be sent to BYU.
Login to your College Board AP exam account and download the .pdf Score Report. Send that .pdf file to
transfer@byu.edu
from your BYU student email.
Contact College Board and pay for your score report to be sent to BYU.
International Baccalaureate (IB) Scores
If you scored a 5-7 on an IB exam, you may be eligible to receive BYU credit depending on when you took the exam.
To transfer your IB scores to BYU, create a
case
and include the date you took the exam and your personal or school code, if possible. We will search the IB database for your records and add them to your account.

name: Transfer Credit Rules and Associate Degree GE Waiver
text:
Transfer Credit Rules and Associate Degree GE Waiver -- BYU Transfer Team rules for how transfer courses fulfill BYU general education (GE) requirements:
Our
unofficial
transfer guides show courses from our primary feeder schools that transfer to fulfill BYU general education requirements. These guides are updated for the current school year and are subject to change at any time.
Completion of the general education (GE) requirements at most regionally accredited institutions will fulfill the majority of your BYU general education requirements. General Education programs with less than 30 general education credits will be evaluated on a course-by-course basis for equivalency within the GE program. Students generally demonstrate completion of the GE requirements with an Associate degree, Letter of Completion, or Certificate of Completion. Please carefully review the transfer guides below.

Completion of the general education (GE) requirements at most regionally accredited institutions will fulfill the majority of your general education 
requirements at BYU. General Education programs incorporating fewer than 30 credits of general education will be evaluated on a course-by-course basis 
for equivalency within the GE program. Students generally demonstrate completion of the GE requirements with an Associate degree, Letter of 
Completion, or Certificate of Completion. Please carefully review the transfer guides below.
If you have completed your associate's degree, you only need to fulfill these BYU core 
requirements.
